title: senecio squalidus subsp. aurasiacus
intro: Senecio squalidus subsp. aurasiacus, also known as the Ox-eye Hawkweed or Mountain Senecio, is a plant species native to the mountainous regions of Europe. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving headaches, and treating wounds. Traditionally, Senecio squalidus subsp. aurasiacus has been used in herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and salves, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
